reviews.review_msg: Dr. K is tough and strict but this is for your own benefit. Her leading her classes in this way ensure you actually stay on top of things because the course is content dense. She's also extremely kind, compassionate and approachable. I think she is probably the best prof in the MBB department. Put in the work, as you should! She's great and fair!!
Dr. K is the analogy queen. She makes you understand.
Do not take, wait for another teacher to teach the class. If not, look for other class alternatives.

Honestly, if you get your money's worth from tuition and actually attend her lectures, she'll tend to *really* emphasize certain things that are examinable. There is a LOT of material in this course, so I can kinda see why she can sometimes seem rushed, but she was very approachable after lecture and really good at clarifying things I didn't get

I had Dr K for 229 and 222. She's the best prof i have had in MBB. Go to lectures and go to office hours. She can seem intimidating at first but she's so knowledgable in her field and soooo helpful if you just go and ask. Can not wait for 309 with her. Take her courses!
She is the best prof I've had in mbb. Go to office hours. Tells you whats on exams if you go to lecture. Take her courses!

Tbh she's not bad. Just really have to memorize every key detail of each lecture. Exams make are entirely your grade so do your best on the first two midterms. Final exam is not culumative even tho she says it is! Focus on the last few lectures and you should be ok.

Lecture slides are very text heavy yet do not have most of the important information on them (she will make additional notes on slides which you will not have access to). She does not record lectures or let you have your phone out to record lectures. So if you miss a day, you miss all the important information. Exams are VERY specific.
such boring lectures.

She is one of the best prof at SFU. Her practice exams are similar to the midterm and the final. She is very caring and understanding. Records her lectures too. I don't understand the bad ratings - if you can't get a good grade with her then maybe consider different major.

The Best Professor in SFU like actually 100%. Her midterms and final exam were based on material from lectures and problem sets assignments. She is so caring for students and very nice person. Her teaching is clear and understandable! Take with her only MBB cours!

First of all, MBB 222 in itself is a very heavy course in terms of learning so much in so little time. Dr. Li definitely eases off the load by giving clear lectures and provide tons of practice material for exams. She cares about her students and wants everyone to do well! For someone who rarely went to lectures I did very well on the midterms.

During my time at SFU, few prof's have stood out like Dr. Li. She's caring, clear, and genuinely wants her students to succeed. Her lectures are straightforward and her exams are fair. If you put in the work, you'll do well. Honestly, the negative reviews seem to come from people who didn't try because she gives you everything you need to succeed.

Dr. Li is genuinely the best professor I've had at SFU. Her slides are clear and very informative. She is so passionate about teaching and is very approachable. She tries her very best to make learning fun. MBB is a tough course since there's so much to memorize, but I'm so glad I took this class with her. Do the problem sets and you'll do good!

The best Professor I have had at sfu. Her lecture slides are great and she explains everything carefully. She provides practice exams and problem sets along with answers keys, all of which are great sources of studying and set you up for success.

prof yueh li is really kind and caring. she genuinely takes into consideration the feedback from students. someone mentioned changing midterm dates but she only did that to be considerate for us!!! practice sets for 'completion' and practice midterms like come on it doesnt get as good as this. She is really helping us, thx Dr. li.

Professor Li is the best! She is so kind, helpful and really wants her students to do well. We are so lucky to have her. Literally the fact that we have access to practice midterms that are pretty much the same as the actual midterm is unbelievable. Just show up, try, do the practice stuff and you are going to do just fine. Thanks professor.
She is one of the best profs I've ever had at sfu. She is super nice and her lectures are amazing. Her exams are really fair, as long as you go to lectures and do the practice exams.

Dr. yueh has been so helpful in this class. I was worried about taking this with another prof. glad we have her teaching us. also, I think the other ratings here with some 'tough' words should probably give their head a shake. accountability starts with ourselves before anybody. Midterm #2 was a fair test and she really guided us well towards it.

Professor Yueh told us up front the second midterm would be harder because of the topics. she didn't try to hide anything. all the practice material and her helpfulness makes this course so much more manageable. Just wrote the second midterm today and I felt really prepared based on her lecture notes detail, support and the TAs in this course.

Dr. Li is very sweet and accommodating (intended to move midterm to the end of the week to give us more time, kept the original date due to feedback, which i think is the source of some of the saltiness here). Slides are dense + frequently updated but her communication/organization of the material + exams is better than other recent teachers of 222
Her slides are riddled with mistakes and incorrect information

read the 222 reviews and tbh Alison is so nice and helpful. dunno what people are saying, probably r not mbb students so wanna get things handed to them. we know that Alison is a god-send for us her expectations and testing is more than fair. I hear other profs who teach this course do not even have recordings or practice midterms. take the win.

Dr. Li is awesome.super passionate, caring, genuinely cares about the students and success. Always trying to keep the students in mind and provide resources to succeed. lucky to have her for 222, the other rates don't know what they are talking about, maybe if they looked at themselves in the mirror it would be better. Would take again for sure.

Regarding Alison Li. Very rushed lectures and course content. She often has to correct herself the next lecture after you have already learned the wrong material. She also is very incompassionate towards people's circumstances. She has even moved midterm dates around multiple times leading to further frustrations. Overall would not recommend.

Her midterm was easy but now she can't even teach us the content properly. Her slides are a mess and she's always teaching with a different version of the ones uploaded on canvas. Too much content that she rushes through and just overall makes this course confusing for no reason.

The lectures are fundamentally horribly laid out and consists of walls of nonsense in an order that does not flow well. Concepts are covered with the grace and elegance of a high school Spanish skit translated back into English and she covers the material by reading straight off the slides with much the same energy.

Spring 2023: Would not seek him out as a prof but he was ok. Slides had a ridiculous amount of information on them and He does not say a single word on the slides during the lecture. exams can have very specific questions as well. His exams were decent but there was a time crunch and tutorials were optional. He ain't that bad just very monotone.

Dr. Paetzel did a pretty good job. The exams were fairly easy, but there was a ton of material to memorize. The lectures were pretty dry at times, but the content was actually interesting. Dr. Paetzel was always available after class to answer any questions students might have.
As I prepare for this second midterm I am realizing this degree ain't for me.

Professor Paetzel is a good prof and fairly accessible since he is always available for questions after the lecture. You can tell that he is very passionate about proteins but the lectures are sometimes dry. Make sure to understand and memorize the slides and you will be set! Also tutorials really help you understand the material.

Dr. Paetzel was never accessible outside of lecs and the TAs weren't too helpful in answering questions. Not enough practice and very heavy lectures, so most of the course had to be self-taught. Exam questions were asked vaguely but required an answer identical to the answer key. Almost an impossible A regardless of how much content you know.

Dr. Paetzel wasn't bad but also not great by any means. His lectures are very monotone and definitely not for everyone, though I didnt mind them. His test questions come directly off his slides - and are either 1) incredibly easy or 2) wildly difficult and specific. Overall, I'd take a course with him again but I wouldn't seek him out as a prof.

Lecture slides are more helpful than his actual live lectures, assignments are literally just regurgitating lecture slides, exams only cover a fraction of the material so you'll waste time studying things that wont even be tested and mark will not respond to any questions. The class isn't too hard but you'll be teaching yourself

His exam questions are usually from the lecture notes and the tutorial questions. That being said, this is not an easy class because there is A LOT of memorization! However, his notes are really good so you don't need the textbook. He is always open to helping people with any questions. Nice guy, he's sort of like a cute grandpa.

MBB 222 in fall 2025.
Cons:
-immense amount of content
-Difficult to know which topics to focus on
Pros:
-Good lecturer 
-Lectures were provided online with visuals and were an excellent resource
-Piazza was an amazing resource and think all classes should have it, Dr. Young and the the TA was very helpful in clarifying concepts and expectations

if you are taking this class to move forward with your major. Do NOT take this class with this man. Wait for another semester. You will be completely overwhelmed. You wouldn't even know how to study for his class. He gives way too many materials, labelling as examinable. His tests are the worst. I repeat, avoid this man.

slides and objective lists very messy. didn't bother reading the textbook bc i couldn't find the pages on my edition. exams are difficult, and i didn't understand where the hw questions came from. even if you memorize everything you probably won't answer the questions right.

Dr Young's lectures are jumbled and disorganized. He is passionate about his work but it translates terribly into lecture slides with lacking explanations. Tests are marked harshly based on certain terminology words. Save your time in lecture and read the textbook and slides by yourself.

Out of all the profs I've had at sfu he is the worst one. lectures were not helpful for me. Exams were extremely badly worded, many people I know lost points for the wording of the questions. Felt like I wasn't graded for what I actually knew unfortunately.

Just don't bother. As a 2nd year student nothing can prepare you to write 45 minute exams out of 80 marks. The lectures are extremely difficult to understand and he does not propose any hints as to what may appear on the exams. The level of difficulty on some of the exams is through the roof, and even harder grading done by the TA's.

his lectures were like a heart attack because he would do practice problems and then ask ppl in class to answer questions, super hard to follow him in lecture so i ended up self teaching the entire course. he provides objective lists which were super useful for that but exams were def hard. lol do not take this w him if u wanna get a good grade

I liked his explanation tbh, he made lectures interesting. But boyy the exams!! The TA's didn't even know what his qestions were asking! His exams were so difficult for no reason. He puts fill in the blank? like dude wth how on earth am I supposed to know what you want me to write. Both midterms were graded wrongly, had to argue to get points.

Lectures are difficult to sit through, and his lecture slides are incredibly crowded and unorganized. His tests are exactly like his quizzes, but it's unclear how to study efficiently for the types of questions he asks. Would not recommend.
horrible prof. needs to get his lectures in check. just cause you understand them doesnt mean anyone else will, also going to lecture is useless and overall worst prof at sfu

going to lecture is a major waste of time but the slides are confusing on their own so pick your poison. he doesn't upload the notes ahead of time which would've been fine if he wasn't so hard to follow. half the time you can't hear him and the other half he rambles on and on about things you don't even need to know. just take it with a diff prof

His office hours were amazing (probs why I got an A+). He asks you questions so you can actually understand the material. His lectures are pointless to go to. Read the lectures, go to office hours, and do objective list (practice test not that similar). His tests require you to very much know the material via lectures and the objective list.

Lectures and slide decks are unclear and confusing--he goes off topic a lot during the lecture. Make sure to keep up with the objectives from day 1; they are like a vocabulary list to understand the heavy science jargon in his slides. His class is quite messy and unorganized. Avoid him if possible!!

Dr. Young is a good prof and cares that his students do well. His slides are messy but they contain all the information needed. He is very clear about the testable material and provides lots of practice. At the beginning of every lecture he did open review on the previous lectures content. I recommend taking MBB 222 with him.
Wanted very specific answers for the exam, honestly didn't learn much in lecture. And you will definitely be picked on in lecture.

I really like Dr. Young. I understand some of the ratings because some of the test questions can be confusing(material is hard). BUT he is a great prof, he will sit at office hours with you and really explain things, he also wants you to understand and be capable of explaining things back to him. He's not a “memorize” type of prof.

I honestly really enjoy his class. He provides clear objectives for each week, and only the items on that list are examinable. His exams are fair, he records his lectures, and he is very accessible. The only downside is that the exams can feel a bit rushed, but overall, he is a very good professor. He is passionate and explains things well.

I really liked Dr. Young, he was always there to help outside of class, his lecture material is bit unorganized but if you actually sort through it all of the information is there and its very thorough. His exams are very fair and if you actually go through all the lectures it's all there for you! I would definitely recommend taking it with him!!
great prof :D he has a high interest in DNA & RNA..especially DNA!! so i guess he's suited to teach this mbb class since 80% of this class is elaborated on DNA and RNA

he is an awesome prof. don't miss out on the chance to have him as a prof! yeah he talked pretty fast, but if you actually pay attention to what he says, the stuff is easy. a lot of ppl said the final was hard, it sort of was :P but i really enjoyed his class!

super awesome notes, but talks a bit too fast during lecture. I found going to lecture made info stick much easier than having to learn on ur own. approachable. SUPER FAIR MARKING and really, there are no surprises. I don't know what people are complaining about. minimal effort got me a B. If u did poorly, u prolly missed the big picture...
Great lectures, easy to understand. Exams are fair
Hands down the best prof I have ever got in my 2 years undergrad experience. Dustin is my kingg!

I highly recommend taking a course w/ dr.king!! Great guy, not a bad thing to say about him. I def recommend that if u can tach ochem1 first, taking them at the same time can be really hard since everything will require at adequate understanding of ochem and many fall behind bc of it

Dustin is the goat. Great guy once you get to know him and always willing to help in office hours. MBB 222 is a dense course but he does well to concise it and try and make the course interesting for students. pay attention to his lectures and slides and you should be good to go. I would take any course with him for sure
Honestly goated prof. Very clear what you needed to know and exams were super fair. Assignments were not too difficult either.
Amazing prof, def would love to take again if possible, hope he teaches more upper level courses in the future.

10/10 prof. Lots of content and lec heavy, but does an amazing job at simplyifying all of it. Final was harder than the midterms, but expected due to a lot more in depth content, still fair though if you studied. Never did anything extra (office hours, tutorting, tutorials,etc.) only listened to lecs and did amazing in the class

There are a lot of notes to take from lecture, so I recommend taking notes on top of the lecture slides if possible. The textbook isn't totally necessary, but helpful if you want to perform really well. The exams are pretty reasonable, but also very very rushed, so watch out for that!

Dr. King is one of the kindest, most knowledgeable, and accessible profs there are at SFU. The bad reviews he gets are because MBB 222 is a really dense course no matter how you go about it. Get your CHEM 281 first to help you survive. I had to put in work for this course and went to my TA's office hours every week but in the end, it was worth it.
He is a very considerate professor and he explains things very clearly. The class itself isn't easy but he's a fair marker so if you study enough you'll do fine.

You need to go to class and actually pay attention because he tends to focus on specific topics that you can't get from the lecture slides alone and those things WILL pop up on the exam. The only thing about his testing is you get 40 MINS for both midterms, and that is NOT ENOUGH but other than that his tests are pretty fair. Don't fall behind

this is the first time im writing a ratemyprof review and its just because dr king was literally the best!! i was initially nervous about taking the class because of the reviews but honestly if you study his slides (know them well) this class won't be too bad. it is content heavy, yes, but dr. king made it really interesting and I loved it!

I took this course at the same time as chem281 and found the first exam quite difficult, as the material requires a decent amount of understanding of Ochem. However, Dr. King explains concepts well and I was able to get my grade up later on. Lots of memorization for a lot of material, and a huge time crunch for midterms.

you can tell this guy enjoy what we studies and teaches. Very good at explaining difficult concepts and even records lectures to look back on just in case. His practices midterms can appear “easier” than the actual midterms, but the only thing i didnt enjoy were the time crunches made for the exams. Other than that, great dude!

Dr. King was an amazing professor. He's very passionate about what he teaches, which allows him to teach the material really well. He tests based on what he teaches and emphasizes, and is really clear on what you need to know

Dr King is a nice guy who seems to be very knowledgeable about the topics, as he answered every question asked in class. The two midterms are very time-crunchy with there only being 40 minutes. The final is the same format, just with 3 hours so it gives you time to review your work. MBB 222 has too much content, but I think Dr King did a good job.

I'm taking chem 281 with this course so I feel like it may distort my perception on him. Honestly, the tests are decently fair but there is so much content and a little more than half an hour to finish the midterms (which i hate) I often have to fight for my grade to get above average so I wouldn't recommend mbb 222 as a co req w/ chem 281

Explains so clearly, great lecturer, very charismatic. Exams are fair enough, though there are sometimes small bits out of nowhere that were not focused on in lecture. Overall great prof, relatively fair exams.

Dr kings classes have a good atmosphere and his lectures can be engaging if you're interested in the subject. the midterms are fair,doesn't have enough time but you can get a good grade if you go over the lecture materials thoroughly. Final was harder than the midterms, more challenging questions. Overall would recommend taking this course with him

He is a great professor, he seems very passionate about what he teaches. The course itself is very heavy on material so you are going to have to memorize a lot of things. His tests are not too hard but you do need to study a lot for them. Overall really good professor.
Excellent lectures and very approachable. If you work hard and commit to learning the material you will do very well in this course. Assessments are very fair and straight forward.

overall a nice person. doesnt know how to teach "everything is testable" practice mts r nothing like the actual midterms just memorize everything in his slides dont even try to understnad cuz you wont.

Prof King is a great lecturer and explains all material very well. Course is super loaded with material and practice midterms are not a reflection of what exam will be like. Exams are pretty tough and require a lot of attention to each MC question.

Did so bad on the 2 midterms but I locked in for the final. All you need is hard work and dedicate your soul to studying. Lecture heavy, too many things to memorize. Overall I think he is good at teaching and would recommend him if you actually put in the work.

Difficult and not good at teaching. Does Not respond to emails regardless of email subject. Tests dont give students enough time and questions are unnecessarily confusing. Overall would avoid if possible.

nice guy but horrible prof. he expects us to memorize so much bc "everything is testable". his practice mts were way easier and nothing like the actual mts so don't think ur ready when ur actually not esp bc his mts are only 40 minutes long with 40 marks.

He is a nice person. However, slides are loaded with so much information and he will never say what to study or focus more on for exams. He will just say everything is testable and then memorizing all that he puts on his slides is hell.

a lot of details to remember, anything in the slides or that he talked about can be on the exam, don't fall behind, and mock exams are usually a lot easier than the real exams. very passionate and likeable guy though

Decent professor that provides lecture recordings and seems passionate about his stuff. Course is densely packed with details so try to remember most of it bc "everything can be tested". 40 minute midterms were very rushed, but the final was not too bad.

MBB is a heavy content course, and while King tried his best to teach it, its hard to stay focused in his class for the whole semester. His exams are 40 mins long which is kinda short for the amount of questions he gives us. 6/10 prof.

Passionate, enthusiastic, and seems to enjoy teaching the course. The course is very material-heavy: lots of information to learn and understand. Midterms are only 40 minutes long which in my opinion, is too short. The extra 10 minutes can help obtain better marks. Slides are very dense, many of them containing unnecessary information.

Lectures are very dense so it can get tough to stay focused the whole time but Dr King does a good job going through the material thoroughly. Very receptive and encouraging to questions during lecture. Tests are very detail oriented and will get very specific.

Lectures may be dry but if you pay attention, he explains things really well. Listen to lecture recordings if you run out of time to study! Read the textbk if you can. Exams were generally not too difficult for me. MT2 was a lil tricky but I expected it to be so as it was all multiple choice (due to strike). I can tell he cares for his students!

professor King is amazing considering that it was his first semester teaching I think he was wonderful. I must say his first midterm was light work and then he DUCKED US UP with the second midterm it was very difficult but then his third was ok. ALL IM SAYING IS ACTUALLY understand everything on his slides thats ur way to an A. His final is fair.

This was Dr. King's first semester as a prof and I really enjoyed his class. You can tell he loves his field. 
I recommend listening back to the lecture recordings and using the textbook as an extra resource. I wouldn't say he's a tough marker but he wants understanding. He tests content taught the class before the midterm but overall he's fair.
Dr. Craig is one of the best. She has very clear explanation of concepts. Her lecture slides are very informative, so you won't need to do much textbook reading. Overall, great prof!

Lisa Craig is a nice prof who'll answer your inquiries via email quickly. Her slides'll have everything you need to know, so you can skimp out on class. That being said-- they have quite a bit of extra information as well, attending would help you sift through what you really need to know. The class is undoubtedly a lot of work but very passable.

Very fair. You don't need the textbook, exams are 100% based on lecture powerpoints. Lecture were really dry, and didn't usually add anything that wasn't in the uploaded slides. Homework heavy (weekly assignments) but it was actually helpful in learning the material. Not hard to get a good grade if you're willing to put in the time

You can tell she's passionate about the course but lectures can be dry and there's a lot of information to absorb but she tells you everything you need to know on her lecture slides. No textbook needed. Midterms were very fair no surprises. Really enjoyed the course with her!
Demanding but very knowledgeable professor that I highly respect.

Absolutely amazing prof. He would put videos on youtube to further explain topics and show examples, and was fully acessible for extra help. Super friendly, but does expect you to work (but only as much as him-which is a lot).
A great lecturer in the MBB dept.  Would recommend to take any MBB class with him.  Not easy, but makes the material clear and concise.

Dr. Chu is amazing. This class, however is pretty hard, but you just need to put in work. He tells you what's important and what to avoid which saves a lot of time, and also has review sessions which is a major key. He actually takes time to know the people he interacts with and if very kind and helpful. 10/10 professor!

He is a fun exciting prof that most people love! He is good at explaining concepts, great at giving you  extra time outside office hours. Seems genuinely interested in the happiness and success of his students. He prepares you well for exams and the exams are pretty fair. Totally recommend him as your professor! :)

I had Eugene for MBB 222 in summer 2015 and he was amazing!! he explained everything really thoroughly, and made sure to repeat concepts that (historically) students had trouble with. His practice midterms were a really good reflection of his exams. He's patient, available, and very open to constructive criticism. hands down best prof ever :)

I had Professor Chu for MBB 222 and he was awesome! MBB can be boring but he really puts in the effort to make the class interesting, and to help you get a good grade, and if youre not doing too good, to make sure you pass. I was in his class fall 2014, the first one he was teaching, and i definitely recommend him over any of the other profs.

ALWAYS available for extra help. I did badly on a midterm and he personally emailed me to check in and invite me to his office hours to go over the material. Tough material, tough exams, tough grading (he doesn't scale), but always fair, always respectful towards students, always obviously happy to be there.

Great professor. He follows all the aspects of a good teacher and if he was doing any other course, would be my most favorite professor thus far.  Mbb 222 is a hard course, no matter who teaches it. Eugene just makes it a little easier.  Power point slides seem to come from the department, but Eugene tells you what to know, and what to ignore.
Unless you have prepared or you have extremely good skill of memorization, don't enroll in MBB 222 no matter what prof you choose.

All of the answers to his tests come from his notes! He is approachable and the grading scheme is very fair. Anyone who did bad in his class has them-self to blame for it. His notes are clear and he explains topics well.I would take his class again because most of the students are whining and getting worse grades giving you a better opportunity
Good lecturer, asks mostly fair questions with a couple tricky ones. Exam/midterms are short so if you make very little mistakes, you'll still end up losing a big chunk of marks.

He is a good person, but not a good prof. He literally spent the last two weeks rushing the material. We literally did 3 lectures in 50min time -- he sped through it SO fast. He exams are hard and picks on tiny details....

He is trying his best, but still, the exams aren't really fair. He tend to pick the tiny parts of his notes to put on exams. Also he taught 50% of material in first 9/10 semester and taught another 50% on the last few days. usually notes are 15-20 pages while the Last notes he spend 2 days on was 47 pages.takes forever to study,fail if ur not smart

midterms are straight from his notes. goes slowly through the material so everyone understands but speeds up a month before the final and does 3 lectures in one class. lectures are usually extremely dull and seem like they last forever. nice guy though and always willing to help. also witty.
Exams are focused on details, not the big picture. Lots of memorization.

Completely useless. I went to talk to Briscoe after class and he completely brushed me off, didn't even answer my question. The test was ridiculous, not based on notes, and he wasted a lot of lecture time. Worst professor I've ever had at SFU.
